zh.lifeSummary: 當Herve de Léon I於大約1070年出生在法國布列塔尼分尼斯艾沙吉波爾-德-萊時，他的父親Guyomarch II de Léon30歲，母親Orwen de Cornouaille29歲。他於1090年之前與Lithuise Adela de Navarre在法國布列塔尼結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1128年3月24日享年59歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Herve de Léon I于大约1070年出生在法国布列塔尼分尼斯艾沙吉波爾-德-萊时，他的父亲Guyomarch II de Léon30岁，母亲Orwen de Cornouaille29岁。他于1090年之前与Lithuise Adela de Navarre在法国布列塔尼结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1128年3月24日享年59岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Léon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Manuel, Carlos, Pedro, Jesus, Jorge, Francisco, Rafael, Miguel, Raul.

Spanish (León): habitational name from León, a city in northwestern Spain, named with Latin legio, genitive legionis ‘legion’, a division of the Roman army. In Roman times the city was the garrison of the 7th Legion, known as the Legio Gemina. The city's name became shortened from Legion(em) to Leon(em), and in this form developed an unetymological association with the word for ‘lion’ (Spanish león).

French (Léon) and Spanish (León): from the personal name Léon, León, from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn. The name Leo(n) was borne by numerous early Christian martyrs and thirteen popes.

variants.name: Len
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Zbigniew, Jozef, Karol.
Polish and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): metonymic occupational name for a flax grower or merchant, from Polish len ‘flax’.
Polish (Leń): nickname from leń ‘lazybones’.

variants.name: Leonides
meanings: Hispanic (Leónides; Mexico): from the personal name Leónides, from Greek Leonidēs, itself most likely a patronymic derivative of Leōn (from leōn ‘lion’; see Leon ) + the suffix -(e)ides.

variants.name: Lyon
meanings: Scottish, English, and French: from the personal name Middle English Lyon, Old French Leon (from Latin leo ‘lion’, or the cognate Greek leōn; see Leon ). Compare Lyall .
Scottish and English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Lyons-la-Forêt in Eure, Normandy. It is unlikely to be from the better-known southern French city of Lyon (see 5 below).

English and French: nickname from Middle English lioun ‘lion’ (Old English, Latin leo), Old French leon, perhaps applied to a brave, fierce, or proud person, or one with a shaggy mane of hair. Compare Lion .

variants.name: Leos
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Pedro, Francisco, Manuel, Ruben, Carlos, Juan, Santos, Jesus, Pablo, Raul, Alfonso.
Hispanic (mainly Mexico): altered form of Basque Leoz, itself a Castilianized form (also Léoz) of a habitational name from Leotz (in Castilian: Leoz) in Navarre.
Greek: from the personal name Leos, a pet form of Leon .

variants.name: Liang
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Jian, Feng, Wen, Wei, Ping, Ying, Cheng, Guang, Jin, Jing, Liang, Chang, Chung, Hu, Nam, Pang, Jang, Min, Neng, Shen, Shiu, Sieu, Yeong.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 梁, meaning ‘roofbeam’ or ‘bridge’ in Chinese: (i) from Liang (梁), the name of a state (located in present-day Hancheng in Shaanxi Province) which was granted to Kang, the youngest son of Qinzhong (died 822  BC ), because the latter made great contributions to the fight against minority ethnic groups in western China during the reign of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After the state was annihilated by the state of Qin in 641  BC , some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (ii) from the second element of the placenames Jie Liang (解梁), Gao Liang (高粱), and Qu Liang (曲梁), names of several cities originally within the border of the state of Jin (located mainly in present-day Shanxi province). In 645  BC , the Duke Hui of Jin (ruler of the state of Jin, died 637  BC ) ceded these cities to the state of Qin (located originally in present-day western Shaanxi province). Later, people from these cities adopted 粱 as their surname. (iii) from Liang (梁), also known as South Liang, the name of another state (located in present-day Longxi in Gansu Province) which was granted to Tang, son of King Ping of Zhou (died 720  BC ). After this state was annihilated by the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), some people kept the name of the state as their surname. (iii) from the Ba Lie Lan (拔列蘭) family, members of an ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surname to 梁 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

variants.name: Leonti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenic, Salvatore.
Italian (Sicily): from the Greek personal name or nickname Leon, Leontos ‘lion’ (equivalent of Latin Leo, Leonis; see Leon 2).

variants.name: Lew
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wing, Yuen, Fook, Jung, Ying, Young, Yung, Bin, Foon, Hong, Kam, Koon.
Polish: from the personal name Lew ‘lion’, adopted as a translation of Leon .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Lev  .

nameMeanings.name: Herve
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally Breton. See Harvey .

en.lifeSummary: When Herve de Léon I was born about 1070, in Saint-Pol-de-Léon, Finistère, Brittany, France, his father, Guyomarch II de Léon, was 30 and his mother, Orwen de Cornouaille, was 29. He married Lithuise Adela de Navarre before 1090, in Brittany, France. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 24 March 1128, at the age of 59.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Herve de Léon I est né environ 1070, à Saint-Pol-de-Léon, Finistère, Bretagne, France, son père, Guyomarch II de Léon, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Orwen de Cornouaille, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Lithuise Adela de Navarre avant 1090, à Bretagne, France. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 24 mars 1128, à l’âge de 59 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Herve de Léon I um 1070 in Saint-Pol-de-Léon, Finistère, Bretagne, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Guyomarch II de Léon 30 und seine Mutter Orwen de Cornouaille 29. Er hat vor 1090 in Bretagne, Frankreich Lithuise Adela de Navarre geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 24. März 1128 im Alter von 59 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Herve de Léon I è nato il circa 1070 nel Saint-Pol-de-Léon, Finistère, Bretagne, Francia, il suo padre, Guyomarch II de Léon, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Orwen de Cornouaille, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Lithuise Adela de Navarre prima del 1090, nel Bretagne, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 24 marzo 1128, all'età di 59 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Herve de Léon Iが約1070年にフランスブレタグンフィニストeアサイント-プオル-デ-ルeオンで生まれた時，父Guyomarch II de Léonは30歳，母Orwen de Cornouailleは29歳であった。1090年以前にフランスブレタグンでLithuise Adela de Navarreと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1128年3月24日に59歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Herve de Léon I(이)가 대략 1070년 프랑스 브레타그니 핑니스트e르 세인트-포-데-e에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Guyomarch II de Léon(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Orwen de Cornouaille(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1090년 이전 프랑스 브레타그니에서 Lithuise Adela de Navarre(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1128년 3월 24일 59세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Herve de Léon I nasceu aproximadamente 1070, em Saint-Pol-de-Léon, Finistère, Bretagne, França, seu pai, Guyomarch II de Léon, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Orwen de Cornouaille, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Lithuise Adela de Navarre antes 1090, em Bretagne, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 24 de março de 1128, com 59 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Herve de Léon I родился около 1070 г. в местности сеинт-пол-де-лeон, финистeр, бретагн, Франция, его отцу по имени Guyomarch II de Léon было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Orwen de Cornouaille, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Lithuise Adela de Navarre перед 1090 г. в местности бретагн, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 24 марта 1128 г. в возрасте 59 лет.

sm.du 1101 au 1128: mai le 1101 e o'o I le 1128

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Herve de Léon I nació aproximadamente 1070, en Saint-Pol-de-Léon, Finistère, Bretagne, Francia, su papá, Guyomarch II de Léon, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Orwen de Cornouaille, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Lithuise Adela de Navarre antes de 1090, en Bretagne, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 24 de marzo de 1128, a la edad de 59 años.
